entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The UK's most common Yaris engine; a compact three-cylinder VVT-i unit prized for fuel economy but known for low-end hesitation.
compatibleVariants: 1.0 Active, 1.0 Icon, 1.0 T2, 1.0 T3, 1.0 T4, 1.0 TR, 1.0 TR Spirit, 1.0 VVT-i
commonFailures: Spark plug wear causing hesitation at low revs (40k-60k miles)
Throttle body carbon buildup affecting idle quality
Oil consumption on higher-mileage units (piston ring wear)
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1KR-FE Toyota Yaris engine replacement

entries.title: 1.33L Petrol
entries.history: Four-cylinder VVT-i engine offering more power than the 1.0L, but timing chain and VVT issues drive replacement demand.
commonFailures: Timing chain rattle on cold start (60k-90k miles)
P0016/P0017 cam-crank correlation codes from VVT oil control valve
Oil sludge buildup if service intervals exceeded
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1NR-FE Toyota Yaris engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: Early Mk1 Yaris three-cylinder engine; simple design but limited power and prone to ignition issues.

Spark plug wear affecting performance

commonFailures: Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption

commonProblems.h2: Toyota Yaris Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Toyota Yaris is generally reliable, but specific engines have known issues: the 1.4 D-4D diesel suffers water pump and head gasket failures, the 1.33 VVT-i petrol experiences timing chain rattle and P0016/P0017 codes, and the 1.0L can develop hesitation from spark plugs or throttle body buildup. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section as a decision tool to compare repair versus replacement costs.

problems.group: Toyota Yaris 1.4 D-4D Water Pump Failure
problems.h4: Toyota Yaris 1.4 D-4D Water Pump Failure - Why Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Yaris 1.4 D-4D Active, Icon, T2-T4, TR, TR Spirit (2005-2011, 1ND-TV)
problems.rootCause: The 1ND-TV's water pump uses a plastic impeller that can crack or seize, causing sudden coolant loss, overheating, and blown head gaskets.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, thermostat, coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before overheating; does not address head gasket risk

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, machine check, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if block is undamaged; risk of recurrence if root cause not addressed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded water pump, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective for high-mileage Yaris; eliminates known failure points

problems.recommendation: On a Yaris worth £1,500-£3,500, a £1,100+ head gasket repair rarely makes financial sense. If the engine has overheated, a rebuilt 1ND-TV with upgraded water pump is the smarter investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1ND-TV diesel engine prices for your Toyota Yaris

problems.h4: Toyota Yaris 1.33 Timing Chain Rattle - P0016/P0017 Code Issues
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Yaris 1.33 VVT-i Active, Icon, T2-T4, TR (2005-2020, 1NR-FE)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear and VVT-i oil control valve clogging cause chain slack, triggering P0016/P0017 cam-crank correlation codes and rattling on cold start.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - VVT Oil Control Valve Clean/Replace
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean or replace VVT solenoid, reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: May resolve P0016/P0017 if caught early; does not fix worn chain

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, VVT components
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause but labour-intensive; viable if engine otherwise healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated tensioner, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage units or if cylinder head damage suspected

problems.recommendation: If your Yaris has under 80k miles and no knocking, a timing chain kit replacement is worthwhile. Above 90k miles or with persistent codes, a rebuilt 1NR-FE often costs less than the repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 1NR-FE Toyota Yaris engine replacement with P0016/P0017 fix

problems.h4: Toyota Yaris 1.0L Hesitation - Low Power & Throttle Issues
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup on the throttle body and worn spark plugs cause poor idle, hesitation from stop, and sluggish acceleration - often mistaken for major engine failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace spark plugs, clean throttle body, reset adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves 80% of hesitation complaints; recommended first step

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Test/replace ignition coils, MAF sensor, O2 sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses misfires if spark plugs alone don't fix issue

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only consider if major internal damage confirmed; rare for this engine

problems.recommendation: Start with spark plugs and throttle cleaning - it's affordable and fixes most cases. Only consider engine replacement if compression tests confirm internal wear.
problems.cta: Compare 1KR-FE Toyota Yaris engine prices if hesitation persists after basic fixes

problems.h4: Toyota Yaris Oil Consumption - Piston Ring & Valve Seal Wear
problems.rootCause: Worn piston rings and valve stem seals allow oil to enter combustion chambers, causing blue smoke, fouled spark plugs, and low oil levels between services.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, valve cover gasket, external seals
repairOptions.longevity: May reduce consumption if seals are the primary cause

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace valve stem seals, decarbonise
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses valve seal wear; does not fix piston ring issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded rings/seals, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for high-mileage Yaris with confirmed ring wear

problems.recommendation: If oil consumption exceeds 1L per 1,000 miles and compression is low, a rebuilt engine is more economical than repeated top-end repairs on a budget supermini.

engineSizes.h2: Toyota Yaris Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement fitted to the Toyota Yaris is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Toyota Yaris 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0L VVT-i three-cylinder (1KR-FE) powered most UK Yaris models from 2005-2020; it's economical and generally reliable but can develop hesitation from spark plug wear or throttle body carbon buildup on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Spark plug wear causing low-rev hesitation; throttle body carbon affecting idle; oil consumption on units over 80k miles
items.cta: Compare Toyota Yaris 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Toyota Yaris 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L four-cylinder (2NZ-FE) was offered on early Mk1 Yaris models; a smooth, refined unit but less common than the 1.0L in UK registrations, with typical wear items including valve seals and ignition components.
commonFailurePoints: Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption; ignition coil failures; timing chain tensioner wear on high mileage
items.cta: Compare Toyota Yaris 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Toyota Yaris 1.33L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.33L VVT-i four-cylinder (1NR-FE) offered more power than the 1.0L across XP90 and XP130 generations; known for timing chain rattle on cold start and P0016/P0017 cam-crank correlation codes from VVT oil control valve issues.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear (60k-90k miles); VVT oil control valve clogging triggering P0016/P0017 codes; oil sludge if service intervals exceeded
items.cta: Compare Toyota Yaris 1.33L petrol engine prices

items.title: Toyota Yaris 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5L naturally aspirated petrol (M15A-FKS) powers the latest GR and standard Mk4 Yaris; a modern, efficient unit with direct injection and no widespread failure patterns reported to date in UK registrations.
commonFailurePoints: No major widespread issues reported; standard maintenance items apply
items.cta: Compare Toyota Yaris 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Toyota Yaris 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4L D-4D (1ND-TV) was the sole diesel option for Yaris in the UK (2005-2011); economical but plagued by a fragile water pump that can cause sudden overheating and head gasket failure, plus a major turbo intercooler recall.
commonFailurePoints: Water pump failure causing overheating/head gasket damage (50k-80k miles); EGR valve clogging; turbo intercooler oil flow recall (2008-2013)
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Yaris 1.4L diesel engines

items.title: Toyota Yaris 1.5L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The 1.5L self-charging hybrid (1NZ-FXE) combines a petrol engine with electric motor and CVT; widely regarded as the most reliable Yaris powertrain with no widespread engine faults, though CVT can feel thrashy under hard acceleration.
commonFailurePoints: CVT transmission wear under hard use; hybrid battery degradation on very high mileage (>150k); inverter cooling issues (rare)
items.cta: Enquire about Toyota Yaris 1.5L hybrid engine availability

engineYears.h2: Toyota Yaris Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Toyota Yaris production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Yaris introduced Toyota's small-car expertise to the UK, with simple 1.0L and 1.3L petrol engines and no diesel option initially.
keyChanges: Launch of first-generation Yaris (P1 chassis) in UK market
Introduction of 1SZ-FE 1.0L and 2NZ-FE 1.3L petrol engines
Basic VVT-i technology for improved efficiency
knownFor: Simple, reliable engines with low running costs
Ignition coil and spark plug wear on higher-mileage examples
Valve stem seal wear causing mild oil consumption
No diesel option in early UK registrations
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2005 Toyota Yaris replacement engines

years.preview: XP90 Early: New Generation, Diesel Arrives
years.description: The XP90 generation introduced the UK's most common Yaris engine (1KR-FE 1.0L) and the sole diesel option (1ND-TV 1.4L D-4D).
keyChanges: Launch of second-generation Yaris (XP90 chassis)
Introduction of 1KR-FE 1.0L three-cylinder petrol engine
First diesel option: 1ND-TV 1.4L D-4D
1NR-FE 1.33L petrol added later in generation
knownFor: 1KR-FE becomes UK's most common Yaris engine; reliable but can hesitate
1ND-TV water pump fragility emerges as key failure point
Early 1NR-FE units show timing chain rattle on cold start
Strong residual values for well-maintained examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2008 Toyota Yaris replacement engines
years.ticker: Toyota Yaris 1.4 D-4D water pump failure • Toyota Yaris 1.33 timing chain rattle • Toyota Yaris 1.0 hesitation fix
enquiries: Toyota Yaris 1.4 D-4D water pump failure

years.description: Mid-life XP90 updates coincided with a major recall affecting 1.4 D-4D models; timing chain issues on 1.33L engines become more widely reported.
keyChanges: Facelift updates to XP90 exterior and interior
Major recall issued for 1ND-TV turbo intercooler oil flow (2008-2013 models)
1NR-FE timing chain and VVT issues gain owner forum attention
knownFor: 39,700 Yaris diesel models recalled for turbo intercooler issue
P0016/P0017 codes become common diagnostic searches for 1NR-FE
1KR-FE remains reliable with basic maintenance (spark plugs, throttle clean)
Used diesel Yaris values dip due to recall concerns
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2011 Toyota Yaris replacement engines
years.ticker: Toyota Yaris diesel recall check • Toyota Yaris P0016 P0017 fix • Toyota Yaris 1.4 D-4D head gasket

years.preview: XP130 Early: Hybrid Debut, Carry-Over Petrol
years.description: The XP130 generation introduced Toyota's self-charging hybrid to the Yaris range while carrying over proven 1.0L and 1.33L petrol engines.
keyChanges: Launch of third-generation Yaris (XP130 chassis)
Introduction of 1.5L hybrid powertrain (1NZ-FXE)
1KR-FE and 1NR-FE petrol engines carried over with minor updates
knownFor: Hybrid widely praised for reliability and low running costs
1NR-FE timing chain and VVT issues continue from XP90
1KR-FE hesitation complaints persist but easily addressed
Hybrid battery longevity exceeds expectations in UK climate
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Toyota Yaris replacement engines
years.ticker: Toyota Yaris hybrid reliability • Toyota Yaris 1.33 VVT oil valve • Toyota Yaris 1.0 spark plug replacement

years.preview: XP130 Mid: Refinements, Hybrid Gains Share
years.description: Mid-life XP130 updates focused on equipment and efficiency; hybrid sales grow as reliability reputation strengthens.
keyChanges: Minor facelift with updated front end and interior tech
Hybrid variant gains more trim levels and marketing focus
No major engine changes; carry-over units with incremental improvements
knownFor: Hybrid becomes most searched Yaris variant for replacement enquiries
1KR-FE oil consumption reports emerge on units over 80k miles
1NR-FE P0016/P0017 codes remain common diagnostic searches
Strong demand for used hybrid Yaris supports residual values
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2017 Toyota Yaris replacement engines

years.preview: XP130 Late: Final Updates Before Mk4
years.description: The final XP130 years saw hybrid dominate new sales; petrol engines remain popular on the used market for budget-conscious buyers.
keyChanges: Final equipment updates before XP210 launch
Hybrid variant accounts for majority of new Yaris registrations
Petrol engines remain strong on used market due to affordability
knownFor: Hybrid reliability reputation solidifies; minimal engine fault reports
Used 1KR-FE and 1NR-FE engines in high demand for affordable replacements
1NR-FE timing chain kits become common preventative maintenance item
Strong UK breaker supply for XP130 engines supports replacement market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2020 Toyota Yaris replacement engines

years.description: The XP210 generation focuses on hybrid and GR performance variants; conventional petrol engines simplified to 1.5L M15A-FKS.
keyChanges: Launch of fourth-generation Yaris (XP210 chassis) on TNGA-B platform
Standard powertrain: 1.5L hybrid (M15A-FXE) or 1.5L petrol (M15A-FKS)
GR and GRMN performance variants introduced with specialised engines
knownFor: Modern engines with no widespread failure patterns reported to date
GR variant attracts enthusiast replacement enquiries for performance upgrades
Hybrid complexity requires specialist knowledge for replacement work
Limited UK supply of XP210 engines keeps replacement prices premium
years.cta: Enquire about Toyota Yaris 2020+ replacement engine availability

faq.h2: Toyota Yaris Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Toyota Yaris engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Toyota Yaris engines typically cost £400-£1,850 supply-only depending on engine code, with 1KR-FE petrol units at the lower end and hybrid or 1.33L units higher. Supply & fit adds £350-£650 labour.
keyPoints: 1KR-FE 1.0L rebuilt: £400-£1,100 supply-only
1NR-FE 1.33L rebuilt: £550-£1,650 supply-only
1ND-TV 1.4L diesel rebuilt: £450-£1,850 supply-only
Hybrid 1.5L rebuilt: £850-£2,950 supply-only
All include minimum 12-month warranty from UK specialists
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Toyota Yaris engine prices for your variant

items.question: Are Toyota Yaris engines reliable?
items.answer: Most Toyota Yaris engines are reliable with proper maintenance, but the 1.4 D-4D diesel has known water pump issues, and the 1.33L petrol can develop timing chain rattle. The hybrid is the most dependable option.
keyPoints: 1KR-FE 1.0L: Generally reliable; hesitation often spark plugs/throttle body
1NR-FE 1.33L: Timing chain rattle and P0016/P0017 codes common
1ND-TV 1.4L diesel: Water pump fragility; major recall 2008-2013
Hybrid 1.5L: Most reliable; no widespread engine faults reported
Regular servicing prevents most common issues
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable Toyota Yaris replacement engines

items.question: What is the best engine for a Toyota Yaris?
items.answer: For reliability, the 1.5L hybrid (1NZ-FXE) is the best Toyota Yaris engine. For budget replacements, the 1KR-FE 1.0L petrol offers the best value with widespread availability and affordable rebuilt units.
keyPoints: Hybrid 1.5L: Most reliable, lowest running costs, but higher replacement cost
1KR-FE 1.0L: Best value replacements, simple, widely available
1NR-FE 1.33L: More power but timing chain considerations
Avoid 1ND-TV 1.4L diesel unless water pump history is confirmed
Match engine choice to your budget and usage pattern
items.cta: Compare Toyota Yaris engine options by fuel type and reliability

items.question: What are the symptoms of Toyota Yaris timing chain problems?
items.answer: Timing chain issues on the 1.33L 1NR-FE cause rattling on cold start, P0016/P0017 diagnostic codes, and potentially loss of power. Ignoring it can lead to valve damage and expensive repairs.
keyPoints: Rattling noise from engine bay on cold start (first 2-3 seconds)
P0016 or P0017 cam-crank correlation codes on OBD2 scan
Rough idle, loss of power, or misfires under load
Often linked to VVT oil control valve clogging or tensioner wear
Replacement timing chain kit costs £550-£950 specialist labour
items.cta: Get quotes for 1NR-FE Toyota Yaris engine with timing chain fix

items.question: What is the Toyota Yaris 1.4 D-4D water pump problem?
items.answer: The 1ND-TV 1.4L diesel's water pump has a plastic impeller that can crack or seize, causing sudden coolant loss, overheating, and blown head gaskets - often requiring full engine replacement.
keyPoints: Water pump failure typically occurs 50k-80k miles without warning
Symptoms: temperature gauge spike, coolant loss, white exhaust smoke
Can cause catastrophic head gasket or block damage if ignored
Recall issued 2008-2013 for turbo intercooler oil flow issues
Rebuilt engine with upgraded pump: £450-£1,850 supply-only
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 1ND-TV Toyota Yaris diesel engine prices

items.question: Does a used Toyota Yaris engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, used Toyota Yaris engines sourced through EngineMarket typically include a 30-90 day warranty, while rebuilt/reconditioned units carry a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists.
keyPoints: Used engines: 30-90 day warranty depending on supplier
Rebuilt/reconditioned: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
Keep service records to validate warranty claims
items.cta: Find Toyota Yaris engines with warranty coverage for your budget

items.question: How long does a Toyota Yaris engine replacement take?
items.answer: A Toyota Yaris engine swap typically takes 1-2 days for supply & fit at a specialist garage, plus 1-3 days for engine sourcing depending on code availability and location.
keyPoints: Engine sourcing: 1-3 working days for common codes (1KR-FE, 1NR-FE)
Fitment time: 5-7 hours labour for experienced Yaris specialist
Hybrid or GR variants may require longer for system calibration
Allow 3-5 working days total for supply & fit from enquiry to collection
Road test and ECU adaptation included in professional fitment
items.cta: Book Toyota Yaris engine supply & fit with UK specialists

items.question: Where can I find a Toyota Yaris engine for sale in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with 100+ vetted UK Toyota Yaris engine specialists, offering rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines with price comparison, warranty coverage and nationwide delivery options.
keyPoints: Compare quotes from multiple UK specialists in one place
All suppliers vetted, rated and reviewed by real Yaris owners
Options for supply-only or full supply & fit nationwide
Transparent pricing with no hidden fees or pressure sales
Secure enquiry process with no spam or unwanted calls

items.answer: EngineMarket's network includes garages offering full supply & fit Toyota Yaris engine replacement services nationwide; simply select the supply & fit option when comparing quotes to include labour.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £350-£650 to engine price depending on location
Includes removal, installation, fluids, coding and road test
All fitting garages are vetted and specialise in Toyota models
Warranty covers both parts and labour when booked together
Save time versus sourcing engine and fitter separately
items.cta: Find Toyota Yaris engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Toyota Yaris 1.33 engine?
items.answer: If your 1.33L 1NR-FE has P0016/P0017 codes but under 80k miles, a timing chain kit repair (£550-£950) may suffice. Above 90k miles or with knocking, a rebuilt engine (£550-£1,650) is often better value.
keyPoints: Repair viable if caught early, no cylinder head damage
Rebuilt engine includes upgraded tensioner and VVT components
Consider vehicle value: Yaris worth £1,500-£3,500 typically
Rebuilt option eliminates multiple potential failure points
All rebuilt units include 12-month warranty for peace of mind
